title: Resources
text:
Open science
I share code, replication materials, and research tooling where possible. Repositories are hosted on GitHub and GitLab; entries below link to individual projects as they are released.




Python


Whisper transcript alignment


Pipeline aligning Whisper ASR output against vendor earnings-call transcripts via dynamic programming, to extract paraverbal features such as silence duration and pause counts.

GitLab →



Python


Earnings-call metadata tooling


Utilities for parsing, indexing, and managing large corpora of earnings-call transcripts (SQLite/CSV metadata pipelines).

GitLab →

title: Joris Berns
text:
Organization Studies · Tilburg University



Assistant Professor in Organization Studies, Tilburg School of Social and Behavioral Sciences


  


Executive communication is not only about what is said — it is about how it is said.
I am a tenure-track Assistant Professor in the Organization Studies department at Tilburg University. My research sits at the intersection of behavioral decision-making, corporate governance, and corporate communication. I study how top managers behave when their organizations face severe threats: how perceptions of such threats form, why executives differ so markedly in those perceptions, and how this shapes their risk-taking and strategic communication.
Methodologically, I work with large-scale computational analysis of text, audio, and visual disclosure data — from natural language processing of earnings calls to paraverbal features of executive speech — often on the Dutch national supercomputer. I am a proponent of open science and share code and materials where possible.

title: Teaching & Supervision
text:
Tilburg University
I hold the University Teaching Qualification (BKO, 2021) and teach across the bachelor and master programs of the Tilburg School of Social and Behavioral Sciences. Since 2025 I also serve as Bachelor Coordinator for the Management and Organization program, supporting the program director in managing the curriculum.

title: Teaching & Supervision
text:
Current courses


Organizing Strategy and Entrepreneurship Master · 2025–present

Advanced topics in management and organization studies. Students work in teams to produce a consultancy report and develop a “serious game” concept.



Academische en beroepsvaardigheden 1, 2 & 3 Bachelor · 2022–present

First-year students learn the fundamentals of academic research and develop competences in presenting, Excel, and teamwork.



Financial Project Management Bachelor · 2022–2026

Fundamentals of project management techniques alongside financial management basics from accounting and finance.

title: Teaching & Supervision
text:
Thesis & PhD supervision
I supervise bachelor theses in the Organization and Management Studies program, and previously supervised master theses in the Strategic Management and International Management programs, as well as bachelor internships. Since 2024, I co-supervise PhD candidate Jingni He together with dr. Francesca Ciulli and Prof. dr. Tine Buyl.

title: Teaching & Supervision
text:
Earlier teaching
Between 2018 and 2022 I taught, among others, Organisatie & Strategie, Cross-Cultural Comparative Management, Strategy for Pre-Master, Creative Entrepreneurship, and Projectmanagement IBA, and gave an invited lecture on Python for the bachelor course Computer Programming.

title: Research
text:
Research program
My research examines the behavior of top management teams under severe threat — how executives perceive threats to their organizations, and how those perceptions shape risk-taking and strategic communication. I combine theory from behavioral strategy and corporate governance with computational methods for analyzing text, audio, and visual disclosure data at scale.

title: Research
text:
Active projects


Paraverbal communication and market audiences

Verbal communication is not only about what is being said, but also about how words are delivered. This line of work extracts paraverbal features — tone of voice, speech tempo, silence — from executive speech in earnings calls, and examines how they shape the judgments of security analysts and investors.



Psychological ownership in the firm

Firm ownership is not solely the formal equity actors possess; it is also about how much actors feel the organization is theirs. This project studies how psychological ownership emerges among organizational actors and what it implies for governance.



Attention under crisis

Crises cause the attention of decision makers to oscillate. Using large-scale text analysis of corporate disclosure, this project traces how managerial attention shifts, narrows, and rebounds when organizations face financial distress.



Status shifts and audience communication

When organizations gain or lose status, expert and general audiences talk about them differently. This project maps those communication patterns and their consequences.

text:

title: Research
text:
Doctoral supervision
Together with dr. Francesca Ciulli and Prof. dr. Tine Buyl, I supervise Jingni He (PhD candidate, since 2024) on the Starter Grant project “Giving and losing sense: How CEOs navigate the disruptive gales of AI adoption.”

title: jorisberns.com
text:
Personal academic website of Joris Berns, built with Quarto and deployed automatically to GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions.



Install Quarto locally (Windows): download the .msi installer from https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/. No R/Python setup is required to render this site.
Create the repository: create a GitHub repo (e.g. jorisberns.com), then from this folder:
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial site"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin https://github.com/jlmberns/jorisberns.com.git
git push -u origin main
Enable Pages: on GitHub → repo → Settings → Pages → set Source to GitHub Actions. The included workflow (.github/workflows/publish.yml) renders and deploys on every push to main.
Custom domain: in the same Pages settings, enter jorisberns.com as the custom domain and enable Enforce HTTPS. At your DNS provider, point the apex domain to GitHub Pages with four A records (185.199.108.153, 185.199.109.153, 185.199.110.153, 185.199.111.153) and add a CNAME record for www pointing to jlmberns.github.io. The CNAME file in this repo keeps the domain binding across deployments.
Retire the old sites: delete or archive the old Netlify project (jorisberns.netlify.app) — it still describes you as a 2021 job-market candidate and competes in search results — and archive the old jlmberns.github.io repo once the domain has switched over.





index.qmd: replace YOUR_SCHOLAR_ID (Google Scholar) and YOUR-ORCID-ID (ORCID) in the identity bar, or delete those two links.
data/resources.yml: the two entries are placeholders — point them at real repositories or remove them.
Verify the two DOI links in data/publications.yml resolve to your proceedings.







To add…
Edit…




A news item
data/news.yml (copy a block, edit two lines)


A publication
data/publications.yml


A talk
data/talks.yml


A code release
data/resources.yml


A blog post
new folder in blog/posts/<slug>/index.qmd


A new CV
overwrite assets/Berns_CV.pdf



Then git add -A && git commit -m "update" && git push — the site rebuilds itself in ~1 minute. Sorting (newest first) is automatic; you never touch HTML.
To preview locally before pushing: quarto preview in the project root.



_quarto.yml          site config, navigation, theme
styles.scss          all design tokens and styling
index.qmd            About / landing page
research.qmd         projects + publications (from data/)
teaching.qmd         courses + supervision
talks.qmd            talks (from data/) + service + grants
resources.qmd        code & packages (from data/)
blog/                listing + posts
cv.qmd               embedded + downloadable PDF
data/*.yml           the files you actually edit
templates/*.ejs      how data files render (rarely touched)

title: A new home on the web
text:
Welcome to the relaunched version of this site, now built with Quarto and deployed automatically via GitHub Actions. The previous version served me well through the PhD years, but a research program built on computational text and audio analysis deserves a website that is itself reproducible: every page is plain text under version control, and publications, talks, and resources live in simple data files.
Expect occasional posts here on research methods — computational text analysis, paraverbal measurement, working with the Snellius supercomputer — alongside announcements of new papers and code releases.
